ogTitle: Chapman Hill vs Cherry Tree Pool: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Chapman Hill and Cherry Tree Pool.
heading: Chapman Hill vs Cherry Tree Pool — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chapman Hill, Western Australia and Cherry Tree Pool, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cherry Tree Pool has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Chapman Hill (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chapman Hill or Cherry Tree Pool safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chapman Hill has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Cherry Tree Pool scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chapman Hill scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Cherry Tree Pool scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chapman Hill or Cherry Tree Pool?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cherry Tree Pool has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
